user_query:
I need a reproducible, cross‑platform demo I can show to my mostly‑Windows colleagues without abandoning my UNIX sensibilities. Please do the following and keep everything in the workspace:

1) Download the Iris data file from the official UCI Machine Learning Repository (dataset title: "Iris", data file name: "iris.data"). Save the raw file exactly at downloads/iris.data. Compute its SHA‑256 checksum and write the hex digest to outputs/iris.sha256 (one digest per file, no extra text).

2) Parse downloads/iris.data into a clean CSV at data/iris.csv with the exact header:
   sepal_length,sepal_width,petal_length,petal_width,species
   Ensure all four measurement columns are numeric and species is categorical.

3) Compute per‑species aggregates and write a summary CSV to outputs/species_stats.csv with the exact columns and order:
   species,count,mean_sepal_length,mean_sepal_width,mean_petal_length,mean_petal_width
   Where count is the integer number of rows for that species, and each mean is rounded to 3 decimal places.
   Also ensure the total row count in data/iris.csv equals the sum of the per‑species counts.

4) Using the recipients listed in input/recipients.csv (columns: name,email), draft a plain‑text email and save it to outputs/email_draft.txt with the following structure:
   - A first line: To: <comma‑separated emails in the same order as input/recipients.csv>
   - A second line: Subject: Iris dataset summary for cross‑platform demo
   - A body that includes:
     • A line stating the source as the UCI Machine Learning Repository (Iris dataset).
     • A line: Total rows: <N>, where N is the number of rows parsed into data/iris.csv.
     • A line: Checksum (SHA‑256): <hex>, where <hex> matches outputs/iris.sha256.
     • One line per species in the format:
       <species>: count=<n>, mean_sepal_length=<x>, mean_sepal_width=<y>, mean_petal_length=<z>, mean_petal_width=<w>
       using the same rounded values as in outputs/species_stats.csv.

5) Internal consistency requirements:
   - The sum of species counts in outputs/species_stats.csv must equal the "Total rows" reported in outputs/email_draft.txt.
   - The mean values shown in outputs/email_draft.txt must exactly match those in outputs/species_stats.csv.
   - The checksum shown in outputs/email_draft.txt must exactly match the contents of outputs/iris.sha256.

Do not include any hardcoded URLs in the deliverables. All output paths must be exactly as specified. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
